“To reduce Inverse Distillation to the standard VSD (DMD-like) formulation, the application of a stop-gradient operator is required to suppress the influence of these auxiliary gradient terms”
SG leads to this connection, which was noted in RSD [5]

This situation is unique to the Masked Diffusion setting because of the SUBS parameterization used in the MDLM model. In general, this property does not hold. We will later demonstrate this difference in the case of Uniform Diffusion .
To pass gradients through the intermediate state, it must be continuous. However, the teacher model was trained on one-hot vectors and may produce poor signals for continuous inputs.
Intuition of IDLM loss: Masked Diffusion
Intuition The model maximize the signal from the teacher model ( attraction ): The model minimize the signal from the fake model ( repulsion ):

Duo parameterization gives Gaussian reparameterization trick
The Duo model can accept points from the simplex as inputs, which makes this possible. As a result, the input is smooth.
DMD-like distillation is ineffective in practice for uniform diffusion. In contrast, using gradients from intermediate states yields a substantially more stable training procedure.
Intuition of IDLM loss: Uniform Diffusion
Intuition of IDLM loss: Uniform Diffusion
It has the same intuition as in IDLM-MDLM, but for distribution at timestep “t”
Intuition of IDLM loss: Uniform Diffusion
Connected with the holding time in the intermediate state

If a generator token has low teacher probability after noising at timestep , the teacher has a short holding time there, which discourages the generator from predicting it. If a generator token has high teacher probability but low fake probability, the generator is even more strongly discouraged from predicting it. ( An attraction/repulsion analogy formulated in terms of holding times )
Overall Intuition of Distribution Matching Paradigm

Capture semantic of sequences: Mixture of distributions
How the model can capture correlation between tokens in factorized regime?
Capture semantic of sequences: Mixture of distributions
How the model can capture correlation between tokens in factorized regime?
Intuition In theory, the generator should sample from the vocabulary space. Thus, during training, its output distribution is encouraged to approach a delta distribution over vocabulary tokens.
Capture semantic of sequences: convergence to the delta
Intuition For each latent variable, we generate a distinct delta distribution over the entire sentence, corresponding to that specific sentence.

Motivation & Overview of Distillation Inverse Distillation IDLM: Theoretical Contribution IDLM: Non-Smoothness of Discrete Space Masked Diffusion Uniform Diffusion Sequence-Level Semantics in few-steps? Practical Aspects Experiments
What latent space do we use in practice?
A strong initialization, as the teacher already captures text semantics and only its input process requires acceleration.
Single-step text generator distillation remains challenging , likely because each latent must collapse to a delta distribution, causing instability.
Inspired by DMD2 [13] , we adopt multi-step distillation to address this issue, enabling the model to generate samples in a few-step regime
We noise it to the chosen timestep
We give it as the input to the model
Training the model for few-step generation allows us to use the same sampling procedure as the teacher model
